message:
Harden Ed25519 context binding, refactor Shamir interpolation, and fix publisher signature verification

ed25519.rs:
- Prepend context length as a little-endian u64 before the context bytes
  in the BLAKE3 hash for both sign_with_context() and
  verify_with_context(). This prevents a class of collision where
  context||message for one pair equals a different context||message split
  at a different boundary. Both sign and verify are updated in lockstep
  so existing signatures remain valid within a single deployment.

shamir.rs:
- Refactor lagrange_interpolate_at_zero() to accumulate numerator and
  denominator products separately before performing a single division per
  basis polynomial. The previous implementation divided inside the inner
  loop (numerator.div(denominator) per j), which is algebraically correct
  in GF(2^8) but numerically less clear. The new form matches the
  textbook Lagrange formula more closely and makes the O(k²) structure
  explicit.

registry/lib.rs:
- Change verify_publishers() to ignore individual signature verification
  errors (UnknownKid, SignatureInvalid) instead of propagating them. A
  manifest may be signed by multiple publishers, and the local node may
  only trust a subset. Previously, a single untrusted or unknown KID
  would abort the entire verification. Now we count only successfully
  verified trusted signatures and check against the threshold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
